raw_response: {"rating": -3.5, "lean": "Left", "explanation": "This is a news report that reads as a sustained critical analysis of Trump's economic record. The author's own prose is consistently dismissive of Trump's claims, frames economic problems as self-inflicted by Trump, and contains multiple editorial judgments that go well beyond neutral reporting. The accumulation of directional framing in the author's own words supports a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes Trump as 'pretending' economic trends don't exist — a direct accusation of bad faith and dishonesty framed entirely in the author's own prose, not attributed to any source; (2) The phrase 'self-inflicted on Trump's part' appears as the author's own editorial conclusion, not a sourced claim, assigning personal blame to Trump for economic deterioration; (3) The author characterizes Trump's administration messaging as 'affordability is an invented issue,' framing it as denial rather than a legitimate policy position, and adds that it 'simply doesn't match Americans' actual experience' — the author's own judgment; (4) The author notes the war with Iran was launched 'at Trump's direction, mind you' — an editorial interjection with no analytical function other than to assign personal responsibility and signal disapproval; (5) The author describes military strikes on Iran as upending 'the always unsteady market,' embedding a negative characterization of Trump's broader foreign policy record into what is presented as economic analysis; (6) The framing of Trump's State of the Union as an attempt to 'tell them, again, how the economy was doing well, whatever they thought they were seeing in their bank accounts' is the author's own sardonic framing, not neutral description; (7) The author dismisses Trump's jobs claim as carrying 'a big asterisk' in their own voice, while Biden-era job growth is described as having 'rose steadily' — asymmetric framing that favors the prior administration; (8) The embedded article link headline references a Democratic congressman framing Trump's Iran strikes as damaging to the economy, an editorial inclusion that reinforces the piece's critical direction."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report that reads as a sustained critical analysis of Trump's economic record. The author's own prose is consistently dismissive of Trump's claims, frames economic problems as self-inflicted by Trump, and contains multiple editorial judgments that go well beyond neutral reporting. The accumulation of directional framing in the author's own words supports a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes Trump as 'pretending' economic trends don't exist — a direct accusation of bad faith and dishonesty framed entirely in the author's own prose, not attributed to any source; (2) The phrase 'self-inflicted on Trump's part' appears as the author's own editorial conclusion, not a sourced claim, assigning personal blame to Trump for economic deterioration; (3) The author characterizes Trump's administration messaging as 'affordability is an invented issue,' framing it as denial rather than a legitimate policy position, and adds that it 'simply doesn't match Americans' actual experience' — the author's own judgment; (4) The author notes the war with Iran was launched 'at Trump's direction, mind you' — an editorial interjection with no analytical function other than to assign personal responsibility and signal disapproval; (5) The author describes military strikes on Iran as upending 'the always unsteady market,' embedding a negative characterization of Trump's broader foreign policy record into what is presented as economic analysis; (6) The framing of Trump's State of the Union as an attempt to 'tell them, again, how the economy was doing well, whatever they thought they were seeing in their bank accounts' is the author's own sardonic framing, not neutral description; (7) The author dismisses Trump's jobs claim as carrying 'a big asterisk' in their own voice, while Biden-era job growth is described as having 'rose steadily' — asymmetric framing that favors the prior administration; (8) The embedded article link headline references a Democratic congressman framing Trump's Iran strikes as damaging to the economy, an editorial inclusion that reinforces the piece's critical direction.

explanation: This is a news report that reads as a sustained critical analysis of Trump's economic record. The author's own prose is consistently dismissive of Trump's claims, frames economic problems as self-inflicted by Trump, and contains multiple editorial judgments that go well beyond neutral reporting. The accumulation of directional framing in the author's own words supports a Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes Trump as 'pretending' economic trends don't exist — a direct accusation of bad faith and dishonesty framed entirely in the author's own prose, not attributed to any source; (2) The phrase 'self-inflicted on Trump's part' appears as the author's own editorial conclusion, not a sourced claim, assigning personal blame to Trump for economic deterioration; (3) The author characterizes Trump's administration messaging as 'affordability is an invented issue,' framing it as denial rather than a legitimate policy position, and adds that it 'simply doesn't match Americans' actual experience' — the author's own judgment; (4) The author notes the war with Iran was launched 'at Trump's direction, mind you' — an editorial interjection with no analytical function other than to assign personal responsibility and signal disapproval; (5) The author describes military strikes on Iran as upending 'the always unsteady market,' embedding a negative characterization of Trump's broader foreign policy record into what is presented as economic analysis; (6) The framing of Trump's State of the Union as an attempt to 'tell them, again, how the economy was doing well, whatever they thought they were seeing in their bank accounts' is the author's own sardonic framing, not neutral description; (7) The author dismisses Trump's jobs claim as carrying 'a big asterisk' in their own voice, while Biden-era job growth is described as having 'rose steadily' — asymmetric framing that favors the prior administration; (8) The embedded article link headline references a Democratic congressman framing Trump's Iran strikes as damaging to the economy, an editorial inclusion that reinforces the piece's critical direction.
